case_title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP

docket_entries.description: MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-27-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
OBJECTION TO PRELIMINARY RESTRAINING ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT WILLICK, MARSHAL S.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERT S. VAILE'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT WILLICK'S COLLECTION EFFORTS ARE IMPROPER AND DO NOT COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1710.15 ET SEQ. PLAINTIFF LIKELY TO PREVAIL DUE TO LACK OF A PROPERLY DOMESTICATED ORDER. PLAINTIFF WILL SUFFER IRREPARABLE HARM WITHOUT THE INJUNCTION DUE TO LACK OF FUNDS TO MEET PLAINTIFF'S NONDISCRETIONARY OBLIGATIONS. PLAINTIFF TO POST AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,000.00 ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 29, 2009. (CCP SECTION 529.) IF HE SEEKS TO CONTEST JURISDICTION, WILLICK TO BRING A PROPER MOTION TO QUASH. COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. NO COMPLIANCE WITH SAFE HARBOR PROVISION OF CCP SECTION 128.7 AND A REPLY BRIEF IS NOT THE PROPER VEHICLE TO REQUEST SANCTIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARSHAL S. WILLICK, AND, MARSHAL S. WILLICK, P.C., AMENDED MOTION AND SECOND AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION; ALTERNATIVELY FORUM NON CONVENIENS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH DENIED. DEFENDANTS WILLICK AND WILLICK P.C. SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION IN CALIFORNIA IN THAT THEY INSTITUTED A WAGE GARNISHMENT PROCEDURE AFFECTING A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT AND HIS EARNING WITHIN THE STATE. HOWEVER, THIS ACTION IS STAYED (AS TO THE ENTIRETY OF THE ACTION) PENDING RESOLUTION IN NEVADA ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS. THE NEVADA PROCEEDINGS HAVE BEEN LENGTHY AND ONGOING, AND THE GARNISHMENT AROSE FROM A NEVADA JUDGMENT. THE WITNESSES, EVIDENCE, COURT FILES AND PARTIES ARE LOCATED IN NEVADA. THE PARTIES ARE SUBJECT TO NEVADA'S JURISDICTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Second Amended Motion And Second Amended Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Subject Matter Jurisdiction; Alternatively Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jan-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARSHAL S. WILLICK, and, MARSHAL S. WILLICK, P.C., Amended Motion and Second Amended Motion to Quash for Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction and Subject Matter Jurisdiction; Alternatively Forum Non Conveniens. Appearances: Eileen O'Brien, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP (213) 680-6400 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants Deloitte & Touche LLP. There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied in part and granted in part. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to quash denied. Defendants Willick and Willick P.C. subject to specific jurisdiction in California in that they instituted a wage garnishment procedure affecting a California resident and his earning within the state. However, this action is stayed (as to the entirety of the action) pending resolution in Nevada on the grounds of forum non conveniens. The Nevada proceedings have been lengthy and ongoing, and the garnishment arose from a Nevada judgment. The witnesses, evidence, court files and parties are located in Nevada. The parties are subject to Nevada's jurisdiction. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction. Appearances: Robert Scotlund Vaile, (707) 833-2350, Plaintiff in Pro Per and Moving party appearing; Eileen O'Brien, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP (213) 680-6400 Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant Deloitte & Touche LLP; Marshall Willick, Esq. of Willick Law Group (702) 438-1195 Appearing thru CourtCall, Pro Per for Defendant Marshall Willick, real party in interest, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction granted. The Court finds that Willick's collection efforts are improper and do not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.15 et seq. Plaintiff likely to prevail due to lack of a properly domesticated order. Plaintiff will suffer irreparable harm without the injunction due to lack of funds to meet plaintiff's nondiscretionary obligations. Plaintiff to post an undertaking in the amount of $5,000.00 on or before September 29, 2009. (CCP section 529.) If he seeks to contest jurisdiction, Willick to bring a proper motion to quash. Court denies plaintiff's request for sanctions. No compliance with safe harbor provision of CCP section 128.7 and a reply brief is not the proper vehicle to request sanctions. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
